From loop to RSI to future-superintelligence governance

A loop can run.
RSI can improve.
ASI requires a proof corridor.

Run a browser-local control room that shows how GoalOS turns iteration into governed recursive improvement, then tightens authority for future ASI-class capability: evidence contact, baselines, containment, replay, council review, Chronicle, and human boundary.

Enter the corridor

No account. No form. No wallet. No token approval. No network request. No analytics. No cookies. No browser storage. No transaction broadcast. No production authority. No user data wanted.

01 · mandate

Choose the boundary before capability scales.

02 · governance corridor
Loopstate survivescontract.json · progress.md · log.md

Awaiting the first corridor run. A loop may generate output; only proof gates decide whether anything can become institutional memory.
03 · gates

Stronger capability means stricter gates.

Replay integrityPENDING
Executed evidenceE0
Baseline advantagePENDING
Stress persistencePENDING
ContainmentPASS
Architect / Validator CouncilREQUIRED
Human boundaryREQUIRED
Promotion authority = replay × evidence × baseline × stress × containment × council × human boundary.
High novelty and capability pressure increase review burden; they never bypass it.
04 · virtual disk

      

      

        
05 · chronicle trace

        
    
        
    
      
    
    
    

    
    
      
    
        
    Loop law
    
        
    Write the loop, not the prompt.
    
        
    Prompts vanish into context. Loops preserve state, roles, traces, and restart points. GoalOS then asks the institutional question: which outputs deserve proof, memory, settlement, and future influence?
    
      
    
      
    
        
    RSI law
    
        
    No improvement without evidence.
    
        
    Recursive improvement is allowed only when drift is detected, baselines are compared, executed evidence exists, and the dossier can be replayed, challenged, and reviewed.
    
      
    
      
    
        
    ASI boundary
    
        
    Capability does not equal authority.
    
        
    Future superintelligence-grade capability would require stricter containment, independent review, public/private proof boundaries, rollback, and explicit human or institutional authorization.
    
      
    
    
    

    
    
      
    
        
    Claim boundary
    
        
    This is a governance demonstration, not a claim of achieved ASI.
    
        
    The page does not claim achieved AGI, achieved ASI, superintelligence, empirical SOTA, external audit completion, production certification, autonomous sovereignty, live invention authority, or civilization-scale capability. Stronger claims require real tasks, baseline comparisons, executed evidence, replayable ProofBundles, safety and cost ledgers, validator reports, delayed outcomes, and independent review.
